title: Boat Rental in Paris Seine: Your 2026 Guide to Costs and Choices
html:
Renting a boat in Paris Seine offers a unique way to see the city, with prices from 80 EUR for a shared tour to 1,500 EUR for a crewed yacht. You can set off from Port de la Bourdonnais near the Eiffel Tower and book a boat online via BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for boat rentals.

Where to set off in Paris Seine

Boat rental in Paris Seine begins at several well-equipped marinas. Choose your departure from Port de l’Arsenal, Port de la Bourdonnais, Port de Solférino, Port de Javel Haut, or the river stations at Quai d’Orsay and Pont Neuf. Each offers easy access to the city’s landmarks. You can book a boat online in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

Select your vessel based on group size and budget. We recommend these four categories: One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

  1. Shared electric boat tour – ideal for solo travellers or couples, up to 6 guests, from 80 EUR per hour.
  2. Small motorboat without licence – best for small groups wanting independence, up to 6 guests, from 150 EUR per half-day.
  3. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Fairline Targa 43) – perfect for a private cruise with up to 10 guests, from 600 EUR for 2 hours. See this motor yacht on BoatHire24 for details.
  4. Crewed catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42) – suited for a full-day party with up to 12 guests, from 1,200 EUR per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Paris Seine?

Prices range from 80 EUR for a shared tour to 1,500 EUR for a private crewed yacht, depending on duration and luxury level. Here is a clear breakdown: You can also see more at browse the listings.

  • Shared electric tour: 80 EUR per person for 1 hour, includes guide.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 150 to 350 EUR, no licence required.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 900 to 1,500 EUR, can include a skipper.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: 1,200 to 1,500 EUR, includes crew and refreshments.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Paris Seine?

For most small boats up to 6hp (electric) and rental motorboats under 10 knots, no licence is required in France. The Seine is a river with moderate current, and operators provide a safety briefing. For larger vessels like the Fairline Targa 43, a valid French coastal licence or an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) is typically required; the operator on book a boat online confirms exact requirements at booking.

Local tip: The best months for calm waters and pleasant temperatures are May through September. Watch for the occasional ‘vent du Nord’ (north wind) in June.

At a glance

  • Price band: 80 EUR to 1,500 EUR
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Trip length: 1 hour to full day
  • Guest capacity: 1 to 12 people
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Paris Seine

Can you rent a boat on the Seine without a captain?

Yes, many small electric boats and motorboats up to 10 knots can be self-driven without any licence.

What is the best time of year for a Seine boat trip in Paris?

The period from late May to early September offers the warmest weather and longest daylight hours, ideal for evening cruises.

How far can you go on a day trip from Paris Seine?

A typical half-day cruise covers about 15 nautical miles, allowing you to see Notre-Dame, the Louvre, and the Eiffel Tower from the water.

Is it possible to rent a boat for a party or event?

Absolutely, crewed yachts like the Lagoon 42 are often hired for birthdays, corporate events, or weddings, with capacities up to 12 guests.

Whether you prefer a quiet electric boat or a luxury motor yacht, BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners. If you have a boat on the Seine, you can browse the listings to see how to join as a host.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Paris Seine and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
